summary: Use AI Agent as a cluster root node to orchestrate tool-using agent behavior inside n8n AI workflows.
description: AI Agent is a root node in n8n's cluster-node AI architecture. It coordinates agent behavior and works with connected sub-nodes such as tools and models according to the documented setup.
why_use_it: Use this node when the workflow needs agent-style orchestration, iterative tool use, or higher-level AI task coordination.
when_to_use: when an agent should decide how to use tools
when tool-calling is part of the workflow design
when the user needs an orchestrated AI root node rather than a simple model call
when_not_to_use: when a single direct model call is enough
when no tool or agent-style reasoning loop is required

credentials.notes: Credentials depend on the connected model and external tools.

templates_examples: official AI Agent templates and examples listed on the node page
related_resources: AI Agent common issues if available
common_issues: confusing direct model invocation with agent orchestration
unsupported_ops_guidance: If the workflow does not require agent orchestration, consider a simpler AI node or a direct model-related node.
limitations: requires compatible connected sub-nodes for full usefulness
more complex than a direct one-step model invocation
gotchas: AI Agent is not the same thing as a standalone model call

selection_rules: Use AI Agent when the workflow needs a root node for agent-style reasoning and tool use.
Do not recommend it when a plain model call is sufficient.
disambiguation: Different from OpenAI action node, which provides service-specific AI functionality.
Different from cluster sub-nodes, which attach capabilities to a root node rather than orchestrate the whole flow.
prompt_hints: build an AI agent in n8n
